local i = 0
  return function ()
    collectgarbage()
    i=i+1
    return string.sub(x, i, i)
  end

local function cannotload (msg, a,b)
  assert(not a and string.find(b, msg))
end

a = assert(load(read1(x), "modname", "t", _G))
assert(a() == "\0" and _G.x == 33)
assert(debug.getinfo(a).source == "modname")
-- cannot read text in binary mode
cannotload("attempt to load a text chunk", load(read1(x), "modname", "b", {}))
cannotload("attempt to load a text chunk", load(x, "modname", "b"))

a = assert(load(function () return nil end))
a()  -- empty chunk

assert(not load(function () return true end))


-- small bug
local t = {nil, "return ", "3"}
f, msg = load(function () return table.remove(t, 1) end)
assert(f() == nil)   -- should read the empty chunk

-- another small bug (in 5.2.1)

assert(a==1 and b==1 and c==2 and d==nil)
a,b,c,d = unlpack(pack(ret2(f()), (ret2(f()))))
assert(a==1 and b==1 and c==nil and d==nil)

a = ret2{ unlpack{1,2,3}, unlpack{3,2,1}, unlpack{"a", "b"}}
assert(a[1] == 1 and a[2] == 3 and a[3] == "a" and a[4] == "b")


-- testing calls with 'incorrect' arguments
rawget({}, "x", 1)
rawset({}, "x", 1, 2)
assert(math.sin(1,2) == math.sin(1))
table.sort({10,9,8,4,19,23,0,0}, function (a,b) return a<b end, "extra arg")


-- test for generic load
local x = "-- a comment\0\0\0\n  x = 10 + \n23; \
     local a = function () x = 'hi' end; \
     return '\0'"
local function read1 (x)
  local i = 0
  return function ()
    collectgarbage()
    i=i+1
    return string.sub(x, i, i)
  end
end

local function cannotload (msg, a,b)
  assert(not a and string.find(b, msg))
end

a = assert(load(read1(x), "modname", "t", _G))
assert(a() == "\0" and _G.x == 33)
assert(debug.getinfo(a).source == "modname")
-- cannot read text in binary mode
cannotload("attempt to load a text chunk", load(read1(x), "modname", "b", {}))
cannotload("attempt to load a text chunk", load(x, "modname", "b"))

a = assert(load(function () return nil end))
a()  -- empty chunk

assert(not load(function () return true end))


-- small bug
local t = {nil, "return ", "3"}
f, msg = load(function () return table.remove(t, 1) end)
assert(f() == nil)   -- should read the empty chunk

-- another small bug (in 5.2.1)
f = load(string.dump(function () return 1 end), nil, "b", {})
assert(type(f) == "function" and f() == 1)


do   -- another bug (in 5.4.0)
  -- loading a binary long string interrupted by GC cycles
  local f = string.dump(function ()
    return '01234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789'
  end)

1583	/// Checks that the chunk mode permits loading the given kind ("binary" or "text").
1584	///
1585	/// C: `static void checkmode(lua_State *L, const char *mode, const char *x)`
1586	fn check_mode(
1587	    state: &mut LuaState,
1588	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1589	    kind: &[u8],
1590	) -> Result<(), LuaError> {
1591	    if let Some(mode_bytes) = mode {
1592	        // C: strchr(mode, x[0]) == NULL  — mode doesn't contain the first letter of kind
1593	        let kind_char = kind[0];
1594	        if !mode_bytes.contains(&kind_char) {
1595	            // C: luaO_pushfstring + luaD_throw(L, LUA_ERRSYNTAX)
1596	            // TODO(port): &[u8] display — lossy UTF-8 here is acceptable for mode/kind
1597	            // strings which are always ASCII literals ("binary"/"text" and "bt"/"b"/"t").
1598	            return Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!(
1599	                "attempt to load a {} chunk (mode is '{}')",
1600	                core::str::from_utf8(kind).unwrap_or("?"),
1601	                core::str::from_utf8(mode_bytes).unwrap_or("?"),
1602	            )));
1603	        }
1604	    }
1605	    Ok(())
1606	}

content.content:
1651	/// Loads and parses a chunk in protected mode, returning the status.
1652	///
1653	/// C: `int luaD_protectedparser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, const char *name, const char *mode)`
1654	pub(crate) fn protected_parser(
1655	    state: &mut LuaState,
1656	    z: ZIO,
1657	    name: &[u8],
1658	    mode: Option<&[u8]>,
1659	) -> LuaStatus {
1660	    // C: incnny(L)  — cannot yield during parsing
1661	    // incnny → state.inc_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1662	    state.inc_nny();
1663	
1664	    let mut p = SParser {
1665	        z,
1666	        buff: LexBuffer::new(),
1667	        dyd: DynDataStub::new(),
1668	        mode: mode.map(|m| m.to_vec()),
1669	        name: name.to_vec(),
1670	    };
1671	
1672	    // C: luaZ_initbuffer(L, &p.buff) — LexBuffer::new() already initialised above
1673	    // (macros.tsv: luaZ_initbuffer → buf.init() / Mbuffer::new())
1674	
1675	    // C: status = luaD_pcall(L, f_parser, &p, savestack(L, L->top.p), L->errfunc)
1676	    let top_idx = state.top_idx();
1677	    let errfunc = state.errfunc;
1678	    let status = pcall(state, |s| f_parser(s, &mut p), top_idx, errfunc);
1679	
1680	    // C: luaZ_freebuffer(L, &p.buff) — Rust's Drop handles deallocation (macros.tsv)
1681	    // C: luaM_freearray(L, p.dyd.actvar.arr, ...) — Rust's Drop handles Vec (macros.tsv)
1682	    // (p and all its sub-fields drop here automatically)
1683	
1684	    // C: decnny(L)
1685	    // decnny → state.dec_nny()  (macros.tsv)
1686	    state.dec_nny();
1687	
1688	    status
1689	}
1690	
1691	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1692	// PORT STATUS
1693	//   source:        src/ldo.c  (1029 lines, ~37 functions translated, 2 omitted)
1694	//   target_crate:  lua-vm
1695	//   confidence:    medium
1696	//   todos:         23
1697	//   port_notes:    13
1698	//   unsafe_blocks: 0
1699	//   notes:         Core call/stack/error machinery translated faithfully.
1700	//                  setjmp/longjmp → Result<T,LuaError> throughout.
1701	//                  relstack/correctstack omitted (StackIdx already offset-based).
1702	//                  Coroutine functions (lua_resume, lua_yieldk, resume, unroll,
1703	//                  etc.) are translated but require Phase E stack-switching to
1704	//                  actually work.  Hook-callback borrow conflict flagged as
1705	//                  TODO(port) in hook() and finish_ccall(); Phase E must solve.
1706	//                  All method calls (check_stack, gc_check_step, get_ci*,
1707	//                  set_ci*, next_ci, etc.) are best-guess stubs to be wired
1708	//                  up in Phase B once the LuaState API is finalised.
1709	// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1710

unk mode. Adding the `AsRef<[u8]>` bound (matching `load_with_reader`) and propagating mode to `l

content.text: Now I'll fix the bug — `load_buffer_ex` ignores the `mode` parameter, so `load(x, "modname", "b")` never validates the chunk mode. Adding the `AsRef<[u8]>` bound (matching `load_with_reader`) and propagating mode to `lua_vm::api::load`.
